concept: Believe / Hold as True / Trust in Understanding
description: To hold something as true or trustworthy, to place confidence in an understanding or a person. 'Nohero' names the inner stance of trust—not blind faith but the settled sense that something can be relied upon, whether knowledge, person, or possibility.
sound_symbolism: The word grounds belief in the body. 'No' begins with nasal steadiness, the self settling. Then 'he' breathes with open aspiration, space made for trust. Finally 'ro' rolls with warm certainty, belief held securely.
grammatical_notes: 'mia thena nohero.' (I believe something.) Clause complements use the 'mena … meno' frame: 'mia mena thia so shua meno nohero.' (I believe that you will come.) Can express trust in persons: 'mia thia nohero.' (I believe in you.) The noun sense (a belief) comes by the event-noun rule, possessor first: 'mia nohero' (my belief), 'lo nohero' (beliefs). Related to 'shelomu' (understand) and 'themui' (faith); shelomu is comprehension, nohero is holding as true.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Belief examined becomes wisdom—we hold views provisionally, testing them against experience rather than grasping them absolutely.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Expressing belief without absolute certainty creates space for dialogue—'I believe' rather than 'it is' invites conversation.
